title: Long-term Status Options for Ukrainians in Poland
title_local: Długoterminowe opcje pobytu dla Ukraińców w Polsce
summary: Ukrainians under temporary protection (ochrona czasowa) in Poland have several paths to longer-term legal status: residence permit for humanitarian reasons, work permit, or eventually permanent residence if they meet the criteria.
steps: Check when your current temporary protection expires
Gather proof of continuous residence in Poland
Obtain a PESEL number if not already done
Apply for a residence permit at the Urząd Wojewódzki (Provincial Office)
If you have a job, apply for a work permit (zezwolenie na pracę) simultaneously
Submit financial proof (employment contract, bank statements)
Wait for decision (3-12 months depending on type)
documents: Proof of continuous residence (registration, rental contracts, utility bills)
Employment contract or work permit application
Proof of financial stability (bank statements for last 6 months)
office: Urząd Wojewódzki (Provincial Office) - your local Voivodeship Office
fee: Free for humanitarian residence permit; zł 350 for regular application
notes: As of 2024, temporary protection for Ukrainians in Poland is extended until August 2025. Those who have been working legally can transition to a standard work-based residence permit. Students can apply for a student visa extension. After 5 years of continuous legal residence, you can apply for permanent residence (karta stałego pobytu).
